ingredients.description: 120 BPM quarter-note pulse
Airport ambience sample for texture
Slow granular chopping: 16 slices over 16 cycles
MIDI-controlled delay send with variable delay time
Mixolydian bassWarsaw line with evolving variations
93 BPM, downtempo lounge
French vocal collages cycled through someCyclesBy
Probabilistic sample switching across 32-cycle blocks
Euclidean bassWarsaw, Bm-A-G-D progression
MIDI-controlled octave effect for voice pitch shifting
80 BPM, mid-tempo breaks
Temptation melodic samples sliced for narrative
Acid superimposition an octave up with MIDI crossfade
Acid synth D-A alternation with rare chromatic variations
Evolving delay time creating shifting rhythmic echoes
160 BPM fast breakbeat/jungle
Ghost samples: 4 haunted variations
Dark chromatic bass with diminished intervals
16-slice break manipulation with alternate switching
Vocal 'self-destruct sequence' drops
104 BPM, dark techno
Storm/thunder bank used across all channels
30+ storm piano samples in evolving narrative arc
Breathing rhythm: 4 hits, 2, silence
Triple MIDI effects stack on piano
128 BPM classic electro breaks
Sci-fi dialogs about electricity, atomic energy, faith
Guitar granularly sliced with complex rhythmic patterns
Long-form Giorgio synth bass with chromatic movement
Global octave bus controlled by single MIDI knob
120 BPM, header: 'Released as: La Fin de l'Insouciance'

Slow bass shifting only at bars 7-8
Dual voice processing: crush + HPF on same knob

8-cycle piano loop chopped into 16 grains

MIDI Leslie speaker on piano for rotary warmth
120 BPM upbeat lounge
Custom sunny pack with bass and brass
Arpeggiated acid on F-Ab-C-G with Euclidean rhythm
Bass staying on root for 4 bars then slowly varying
Dual sub-octave on same knob for massive low end
120 BPM, opens with 'À Mon Amour'
Rhadamanthe piano split into begin/mid/end narrative
Piano surgically split into 3 cue points
Cello pluck B-A oscillation resolving to E
MIDI live tempo from 60 to 180 BPM — real-time manipulation
138 BPM fast breaks
The Lady — vocal chopped into 16 grains over 8 cycles
Triple probability: reversal, stereo micro-loops, euclidean masking

Bitcrush on Giorgio pad for lo-fi harmonic layer

'I'm from Cardiff!', 'Pints' — grime vocals
